name: depthwise-conv

interface_signals.name: data_out
interface_signals.role: one packed output pixel per beat

fit_constraints.bram_formula: Depthwise weights occupy C * KH * KW bytes; for MobileNetV2 the worst case is ~576 channels * 9 = 5184 bytes, trivially fits one BRAM18. Activations are streamed via a per-channel line buffer.
buffer_sizing_rules: Public bus is identical to flat-bus: one packed pixel ([N, C, h, w]) per beat.
Internal line buffer is per-channel: store KH-1 input rows so the next row can complete the 3x3 window per channel without cross-channel mixing.
Weight memory is addressed [c, kh, kw] -- each channel reads its own 9-tap filter.
No cross-channel adder tree: each output[c] depends only on input[c] and weight[c].

protocol_rules: Only layers with groups == input_channels == output_channels select this contract; other group configurations are routed elsewhere.
The output channel count equals the input channel count (no reduction across channels).
Kernel sizes 3x3 and 5x5 are the common MobileNet shapes; the contract does not constrain the spatial kernel beyond what coord_scheduler supports.
Stride and padding are taken from the LayerIR; depthwise variants commonly use stride=2 to halve the spatial resolution.
